I will start by sear
### Identify strategic geographic choke points, military positio
Okay, I will now focus on identifying strategic geographic choke points, military positioning, and territorial vulnerabilities in the Iranian theater for 2026. I will use web search and deep search to gather information, extract verbatim text, and post each finding with its source URL.

## EXTRACTION MODE RULES (MANDATORY):
1. Do NOT summarize findings. Store the VERBATIM text from each source.
2. For EVERY fact found, make a SEPARATE [TOOL: post_finding(verbatim text. Source: URL)] call.
3. Include the EXACT source URL in every post_finding call.
4. After storing all facts, write a brief 2-3 line summary for the plan runner to track progress.
5. You are FORBIDDEN from synthesizing or drawing conclusions. Just extract and store raw facts.

Snippet: 1 day ago -Iran crowdsourced solutions to the worm and is purportedly now better positioned in terms of cyber warfare technology. No government claimed responsibility for the worm. The cyber-worm was also used against North Korea. Iranian cyber defense system - digital fortress part of national information network (national internet) - is developed for thwarting attacks and engaging attackers.

Title: Cyber Operations as Iran’s Asymmetric Leverage - The Soufan Center
URL: https://thesoufancenter.org/intelbrief-2026-march-17/
Snippet: 4 days ago -The cyber operations during the opening salvo of Operation Epic Fury and Roaring Lion moved beyond intelligence preparation: mobile towers near key government institutions were disabled to cut communications, and the BadeSaba prayer application, used by millions of Iranians to confirm prayer times, was used in a psychological operation to rile people up against the regime — seemingly to no great effect. Further on the escalation ladder, Israel kinetically struck Iran’s cyber warfare headquarters in eastern Tehran, according to the Israeli Defense Forces, degrading the centralized command of one of the most active cyber powers in the Middle East.

Title: Iranian Use of Cybercriminal Tactics in Destructive Cyber Attacks: 2026 Updates
URL: https://www.halcyon.ai/ransomware-alerts/iranian-use-of-cybercriminal-tactics-in-destructive-cyber-attacks-2026-updates
Snippet: 2 weeks ago -Hacktivist Operational Silence Suggests Active Campaign Execution: Handala, a pro-Palestinian hacktivist group, has shown markedly reduced public blog activity since January 2026, a pattern historically consistent with active operational tempo rather than dormancy, suggesting ongoing but undisclosed targeting activity. Intelligence Operations Target Population-Scale Data Sets: Iranian APT groups including APT34, APT35, APT39, and APT42 are actively targeting entities holding large individual-level data sets, including ISPs, medical systems, and telecommunications providers, with the assessed intent of identifying and locating regime dissidents.
